name: Passive hyperthermia
summary: Passive hyperthermia is heat exposure without exercise, normalized for human heat-shock and chaperone biomarker response.
mechanism_summary: Raises body temperature through passive heat exposure to test HSP70-linked proteostasis markers in circulating immune cells.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: Active and passive hyperthermia HSP70 PBMC study; Active and passive hyperthermia did not significantly increase PBMC HSP70.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
